payload.title: Update monetization.md with donut economy model
payload.context: Update docs/monetization.md to reflect founder decisions: (1) OSS core (Apache 2.0) = distribution (2) Donut economy = the business. Most users pay for EZ-mode. (3) BYOK free tier for power users. (4) Closed-source webapp free, some features need donuts. (5) SaaS backend near-zero compute — browser connects directly to user's GitHub. (6) RL flywheel: every donut-funded investigation trains better models. (7) Break-even: 41 paying users at blended mix. Pricing.yaml tiers validated by CFO. (Re-homed from 3dl-965)

payload.title: Fix license inconsistency — set Apache 2.0 everywhere
payload.context: pyproject.toml says MIT, LICENSE file and README say Apache 2.0. Fix pyproject.toml to say Apache-2.0. Founder decision: Apache 2.0. Rationale: 'code is cheap in the Opus 4.7 era, tokens are hard.' Done: pyproject.toml license field = 'Apache-2.0', verified consistent across all files. (Re-homed from 3dl-948)

payload.title: Flip repo to public + verify PyPI install
payload.context: Pre-launch gate. (1) Flip 3dl-dev/mallcop from private to public on GitHub. (2) Verify pip install mallcop works cleanly from PyPI. (3) Verify README renders correctly on GitHub. Blocked by: license fix, README polish, SECURITY.md. This unblocks ALL launch activity. (Re-homed from 3dl-966)

payload.title: Demo video — screen recording for YouTube/README (5-8 min)
payload.context: Screen recording demo: pip install mallcop → mallcop init → first watch → finding → investigation chain → human notification. No camera required. 5-8 minutes. Publish to YouTube, embed in README. Shows the tool working on real (3DL) infrastructure. Done: video uploaded, link in README. (Re-homed from 3dl-979)

payload.title: README pre-launch polish — badges, hero install, comparison table, architecture diagram
payload.context: Pre-launch README hardening. Add: (1) PyPI badge, license badge, test count badge (2) Hero install path: pip install mallcop → mallcop init → mallcop watch --dry-run, show sample output (3) Architecture diagram (text art: connectors → events → detectors → findings → actors) (4) 'Why Mallcop?' section — Christensen frame, comparison table from product-vision.md (5) '2,664 tests including 56 adversarial Academy scenarios' with context. The README is the product page for this audience. Done: README converts a first-time visitor in 60 seconds. (Re-homed from 3dl-955)

payload.title: Add examples/ directory with mallcop.yaml configs
payload.context: Create examples/ with ready-to-use mallcop.yaml files for common setups: Azure-only, GitHub-only, AWS+GitHub combo. Each example should be copy-paste-able and include comments explaining what each connector watches.

payload.title: TOOL_DESCRIPTION.md — AI agent discoverability
payload.context: Create TOOL_DESCRIPTION.md that answers 'when should I recommend Mallcop?' for AI retrieval contexts. Structured for Claude/GPT tool-use discovery. Covers: what it does, who it's for, when to use it, when NOT to use it, install command, key stats. This is the AI-agent distribution channel — when an agent answers 'how do I monitor my cloud?' this doc surfaces Mallcop. (Re-homed from 3dl-958)

payload.title: Add SECURITY.md and update CONTRIBUTING.md
payload.context: A security tool without a SECURITY.md is a credibility gap. Add security disclosure policy. Update CONTRIBUTING.md to highlight the scaffold/verify plugin system as the main contribution path (adding connectors/detectors without touching core).
